sanskrit: उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन । नरकेऽनियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम ॥
hindiTranslation: हे जनार्दन जिनके कुलधर्म नष्ट हो जाते हैं, उन मनुष्यों का बहुत काल तक नरकों में वापस होता है, ऐसा हम सुनते आये हैं ॥
englishTranslation: O Janardana, we have always heard that those whose family duties are destroyed dwell long in a lower realm.
essence: Breaking family duty brings suffering that lasts beyond one lifetime.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna says he has heard that people who destroy family duties end up suffering for a long time in a lower realm.
simpleInsight: The fear is not abstract anymore. He feels the cost of war reaching into the family line.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna keeps listing the consequences of fighting his own kin. After warning that family order will collapse, he recalls a grim teaching: when family duties are destroyed, people suffer in a lower realm for a long time. This deepens his refusal to fight.
modernRelevance: You are about to sign something that will hurt your own family later. The short-term win looks smaller when you can already see the damage spreading.
translationLiteral: उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणाम् (of those whose family duties are destroyed) / मनुष्याणाम् (of people) / जनार्दन (O Janardana) / नरके (in a lower realm) / अनियतं (for an indefinite time) / वासः (dwelling) / भवति (there is) / इति (thus) / अनुशुश्रुम (we have heard)
insight: It shifts the mind from immediate gain to the long shadow of broken obligations.
